dealKiller: The FuelEU pooling broker-of-record market is already commoditised in Piraeus by Mar 2026: Kickster (Piraeus office, 2nd Merarchias 11) onboarded 100+ companies / 500+ vessels within weeks of its Mar 2026 Milan-Athens launch; OceanScore Pooling Marketplace serves 100+ customers / 2,500+ vessels and has a Greek office; STX/Vertis (MiFID II since 1998, acquired by STX 2021) launched a FuelEU compliance solution with Azolla in Nov 2024 covering both EUA brokerage and pooling. BSM Hellas (80+ vessels managed from Piraeus, 20-year anniversary Jun 2025) has 'FuelEU fully integrated into emission management services.' The €20K/vessel/yr to €2K/vessel/yr arbitrage Aktis claims has already been priced in — OceanScore Pool-Price Index dropped from €400/t (Jan 2025) to €170-196/t (Mar 2026). There is no incumbent gap to occupy.
patternMatches: Kickster FuelEU Marketplace — launched Mar 5 2026 Milan/Athens; 100+ companies, 500+ vessels in weeks; Piraeus office; subscription pooling platform — exact Aktis wedge, already shipped
OceanScore Pooling Marketplace — 100+ customers, 2,500+ vessels, Greek office, KYC + counterparty matching + execution as managed service; OPX index live
STX Group / Vertis Environmental Finance — MiFID II regulated since pre-2018, acquired by STX 2021; FuelEU compliance solution with Azolla launched Nov 2024; full EUA brokerage and pooling stack
ZeroNorth + Veracity by DNV — integrated EU MRV / EU-ETS / FuelEU service launched May 2025 with Teekay, now rolled out to multiple customers
BSM Hellas — 80+ vessels managed from Piraeus, FuelEU fully integrated into emission management services as of 2025; Arcadia chairs ABS Hellenic Committee
BIMCO FuelEU clauses — Time Charter (2024), MoA (Dec 2025), S&P clauses adopted; pooling allocation now boilerplate contract language, not bespoke brokerage
unitEconomicsConcern: €300-700K/yr ACV per sub-30-vessel owner implies €15-30K/vessel/yr. With Kickster subscription pricing and OceanScore managed service already commoditising the workflow well below this, and pooling spreads compressing 60% YoY (€400→€170/t), the broker margin collapses. The €5-15M EUA float thesis is also weak: Vertis/STX, Hartree, Engie, BP and ICE Endex clearing members deploy 100-1000x that capital at lower cost-of-funds; family balance sheet is a rounding error, not a moat.
loadBearingAssumption: That sub-30-vessel Piraeus owners are a real segment buying differently from large fleets. Falsified: Procopiou (Centrofin/Dynacom/Dynagas/Sea Traders) actually operates ~91 vessels with 88 more on order; Thenamaris (Pantheon affiliate) approaches 100 vessels; Eletson restructured 2024 with ~25 tankers but Greek family fleets cluster via related entities. Compliance is bought at the group level, in-house, by trusted Piraeus staff — Greek family-business literature confirms financially sensitive decisions stay inside the family office, not outsourced to a third-party broker-of-record.
alternativeThesis: none — kill cleanly. Every defensibility leg fails: (a) relationships — Kickster, OceanScore, BSM already sit in Piraeus with Greek-language sales motions; (b) capital float — incumbents Vertis/STX/Hartree/Vitol/Trafigura have orders-of-magnitude more EUA inventory at lower CoC; (c) regulatory licence — MiFID II EUA broker registration is achievable but a dozen incumbents already hold it; (d) AI-native unit cost — ZeroNorth, OceanScore, BetterSea, DNV Emissions Connect already automate noon-report → MRV → FuelEU → EUA workflows. The 'SOR-ships-in-12-months' filter that killed 5 of 7 earlier candidates applies cleanly here too — Aktis was misclassified as surviving.
